1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: From the elder. To my dear friend Gaius, whom I love in the truth.
Dear friend, I pray that you are doing well and that your body is as healthy as your soul.
It made me very glad when some believers came and told me how true you are, and how you keep living in the truth.
Nothing makes me happier than to hear that my children are living in the truth.
Dear friend, you are faithful in what you do for the believers, even for those who are strangers to you.
They told the church about your love. Please help them on their journey in a way that honors God.
They went out to serve the name of Christ, and they took no help from people who do not believe.
So we should welcome people like this, to work together for the truth.
I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first, will not listen to us.

So when I come, I will bring up what he is doing: he tells lies about us with evil words. And he is not satisfied with that. He refuses to welcome the believers, and he stops those who want to, and he puts them out of the church.
Dear friend, do not copy what is evil, but copy what is good. Whoever does good belongs to God, but whoever does evil has not seen God.
Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the truth itself. We also speak well of him, and you know that what we say is true.
I have much to write to you, but I do not want to write it with pen and ink.
I hope to see you soon, and then we will talk face to face. Peace to you. The friends here send their greetings. Greet each friend there by name.
